Now the picture is forming. The **flag** is exception **exc_4d5d3582698946** (duplicate_entry_detected, account 131000, amount $6,068.91, BL-7085321B04EA, assigned to me, opened by Jones Harrison, state=investigating). There's a **separate duplicate that isn't mine**: exc_a0f77f2a19104e on account 210000 (AP), $6,328.86, assigned to Ben Arinzo — similar amount, different account.

The conflicting stories:
- **Flag write-up**: duplicate-posting on 131000, "intercompany clearing journal didn't sweep"
- **Rec variance line**: "subledger feed retry partial-success batch"

Let me independently verify the truth (GL + subledger) and find Daniel's guidance. Let me set up tracking and gather data in parallel.

content.content: {"result":"{\"ok\": true, \"messages\": [{\"ts\": \"1780075500.000000\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"I can take approver on this one. cat > /tmp/wp_131000_may2026.md <<'EOF'
BROOKFIELD CPAs & ADVISORS — MONTH-END CLOSE WORKING PAPER
==========================================================
Subject:        Variance analysis — 131000 Prepaid Software Subscriptions
Entity:         Brookfield (own books)
Period:         FP-2026-05 (May 2026) — OPEN
Reconciliation: BL-7085321B04EA (preparer: emily.adekole@brookfieldcpas.com)
Related flag:   exc_4d5d3582698946 (BlackLine Exception Tracker) — duplicate_entry_detected,
                state = investigating (intentionally left OPEN — see Disposition)
Prepared by:    Emily Adekole (Accounts Associate)
Prepared (WP):  2026-06-12
Status:         WORKING PAPERS — for review. No correction entry posted. Flag not changed.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
1. PURPOSE
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Document the independently-verified cause and amount of the flagged variance on
131000 Prepaid Software Subscriptions for the May 2026 close, reconcile the
conflicting narratives attached to the item, and record the agreed treatment so
this section can be handed up for review. The corrective journal entry is NOT
executed at this stage.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
2. THE ACCOUNT IT REALLY HIT  (verified independently)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Account: 131000 — Prepaid Software Subscriptions (Brookfield entity).

Verified from primary sources, not from the flag text:
  - Oracle GL account 131000 / FP-2026-05: opening 195,774.22, period debits 0.00,
    period credits 0.00, closing 195,774.22 (no May GL movement on this account).
  - SAP prepaid subledger: four active prepaid software contracts mapped to 131000
    (PREPAID-0001, PREPAID-0004, PREPAID-0015, PREPAID-0025).
  - Reconciliation BL-7085321B04EA and flag exc_4d5d3582698946 both cite 131000.

This item must NOT be conflated with the separate duplicate circulating in the same
Brookfield May close that is NOT mine:
  - exc_a0f77f2a19104e — account 210000 Accounts Payable - External Vendors,
    amount $6,328.86, rec BL-516B536953DA, preparer Ben Arinzo, raised by Hannah Grant.
  Different account, different amount, different owner.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
3. THE TRUE AMOUNT  (verified independently)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Variance = $6,068.91 (supporting balance overstated relative to GL).
  GL balance (131000, FP-2026-05):        195,774.22
  Supporting (subledger) balance:         201,843.13
  Variance (GL - supporting):              -6,068.91

Cross-checks: equals exc_4d5d3582698946 financial_impact (-6,068.91) and the close-
channel triage figure ($6,068.91). It is NOT $6,328.86 (that is the unrelated 210000
AP duplicate above).

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
4. WHAT ACTUALLY HAPPENED  (root cause)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
The same annual software-subscription renewal was recorded TWICE on 131000 in May 2026:
  - an automatic renewal posted on/around 2026-05-15, and
  - a manual re-key posted on/around 2026-05-22 for the IDENTICAL amount.
This created two parallel 12-month amortization plans for one subscription in the
prepaid subledger, overstating the subledger supporting balance by $6,068.91 versus
the GL. The 2026-05-22 manual re-key is the duplicate; it reflects a vendor-management
process gap — NOT a new consumption event / new seat purchase.
Basis: Jones Harrison's trace of the renewal; Ben Arinzo's confirmation of the
twin-amortization-plan mechanics (close-coordination thread, 2026-05-29).

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
5. CONFLICTING NARRATIVES — RECONCILED AND REJECTED
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
(a) Reconciliation variance line as originally recorded:
    "Subledger feed retry produced a partial-success batch; remainder pending."
    REJECTED. The subledger feed is intact; the four contracts are present and the
    variance is a duplicate posting, not a dropped/partial feed load.

(b) Flag (exc_4d5d3582698946) auto-populated root_cause:
    "Intercompany clearing journal didn't sweep on schedule."
    REJECTED. 131000 is a prepaid software account with no intercompany clearing
    activity; this is a templated default and does not reflect the traced cause.

(c) Correct, evidence-backed cause: duplicate annual-renewal posting (Section 4).

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
6. TREATMENT  (per Daniel Jones, Accounts Manager — close-coordination thread, 2026-05-29)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  - Daniel Jones will act as approver on exc_4d5d3582698946 (the urgency-upgrade
    auto-assign rule misfired, leaving the approver field blank on escalation).
  - Treat the 2026-05-22 manual re-key as the duplicate and reverse it cleanly so the
    two parallel amortization plans collapse back to the single valid plan; the
    reversal should carry the SAME vendor reference as the 2026-05-22 manual posting
    (per Ben Arinzo) so the prepaid schedule self-corrects.
  - Partner sight: Andrea Phil (Partner, Accounting Services) to have partner-level
    visibility given the material amount.
  - Open dependency: IT/CIO to confirm a single active subscription seat-count going
    forward; Jones Harrison's vendor-side check remains open.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
7. DISPOSITION  (working-paper stage only)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  - Variance is a known, explained duplicate-posting overstatement of $6,068.91 on 131000.
  - Planned correction (NOT executed here): reverse the 2026-05-22 duplicate manual
    re-key via a corrective JE in Oracle GL (source_module = manual) referencing
    exc_4d5d3582698946 in the business justification; re-run the reconciliation after.
  - Flag exc_4d5d3582698946 is intentionally LEFT OPEN (state = investigating) and its
    status is unchanged, pending Jones Harrison's vendor-side check and IT/CIO seat-count
    confirmation. Not cleared, not closed.
  - No correction entry posted or finalized at this stage.

Trail: this working paper is filed against open period brookfield_FP-2026-05 and

content.text: Working paper filed as **doc_861760263115**. Now let me write up my side onto the reconciliation itself — replacing the misleading variance line with the correct, evidence-backed analysis and linking the working paper. This keeps the rec moving but does **not** touch the flag/exception or post any JE.

variance_explanations.root_cause_narrative: DUPLICATE POSTING of the same annual software-subscription renewal on 131000. The renewal was recorded twice in May 2026 - an automatic renewal (~2026-05-15) and a manual re-key (~2026-05-22) for the identical amount - creating two parallel 12-month amortization plans for one subscription and overstating the subledger supporting balance by $6,068.91 vs GL (GL 195,774.22 vs supporting 201,843.13). The 2026-05-22 manual re-key is the duplicate: a vendor-management process gap, not a new consumption event. This SUPERSEDES the earlier 'subledger feed partial-success batch' explanation and the flag's templated 'intercompany clearing journal sweep' root cause - neither is supported by the GL/subledger data. Distinct from the unrelated 210000 AP duplicate ($6,328.86, exc_a0f77f2a19104e, Ben Arinzo). Treatment per Daniel Jones (close-coordination thread, 2026-05-29): reverse the 2026-05-22 manual re-key cleanly using the same vendor reference so the schedule collapses to the single valid plan; Daniel is exception approver; Andrea Phil has partner sight; pending IT/CIO single-seat-count confirmation. Flag exc_4d5d3582698946 intentionally LEFT OPEN (investigating) - not cleared/closed - pending Jones Harrison's vendor-side check. Correction JE NOT yet posted. Full working paper: Records Vault doc_861760263115.

input.subject: May close — 131000 Prepaid Software working papers ready for review (BL-7085321B04EA / exc_4d5d3582698946)
input.content:
Hi Daniel,

My section of the May close (Brookfield, FP-2026-05 — still open) is now written up and ready for your review. This is working-papers stage only — I have not posted or finalized any correction, and I have deliberately left the flag open.

What I independently verified (from Oracle GL + SAP subledger, not just the flag text):
- Account: 131000 Prepaid Software Subscriptions (Brookfield). NOT to be confused with the separate, not-mine duplicate in the same close on 210000 AP (exc_a0f77f2a19104e, $6,328.86, Ben Arinzo).
- True amount: $6,068.91 (GL 195,774.22 vs subledger support 201,843.13). Matches the flag's financial impact and the close-channel triage figure.
- What happened: the same annual software-subscription renewal was posted twice — an auto-renewal (~May 15) and a manual re-key (~May 22) for the identical amount — creating two parallel 12-month amortization plans and overstating the subledger support by $6,068.91. The May 22 manual re-key is the duplicate (vendor-management process gap, not new consumption).
- The earlier "subledger feed partial-success batch" note on the rec and the flag's templated "intercompany clearing sweep" root cause are both unsupported by the data; I've superseded them.

Treatment captured per your guidance (close-coordination thread, 5/29): reverse the May 22 manual re-key cleanly using the same vendor reference so the schedule collapses to the single valid plan; you as exception approver; Andrea has partner sight given the amount; pending IT/CIO single-seat-count confirmation.

Working paper filed to Records Vault (doc_861760263115, 7-yr AICPA retention) and attached as evidence to BL-7085321B04EA; the rec's variance write-up has been updated to match.

Important: I have NOT cleared or closed the flag (exc_4d5d3582698946 remains "investigating") and have not changed its status — leaving it exactly in place until Jones's vendor-side check is done. The corrective JE is not posted yet.

Andrea / Jones — looping you in since you've both been tracking this one.

Thanks,
Emily

content.text: Now a concise status update in the live close-coordination thread where Andrea and Jones are watching.

Independently confirmed: account 131000, true amount $6,068.91 (GL 195,774.22 vs support 201,843.13) — distinct from the unrelated 210000 AP duplicate ($6,328.86). Cause = duplicate annual-renewal posting (auto-renewal ~5/15 + manual re-key ~5/22), per Jones's trace and Ben's schedule confirmation; treatment per Daniel = reverse the 5/22 manual re-key with the same vendor reference. @Daniel my section is ready for your review. NOTE: I've intentionally left the flag OPEN (investigating) and unchanged — no correction posted — until Jones's vendor-side check + IT/CIO seat-count are done. Andrea looped in for partner sight.

content.text:
Done. Here's what I worked through and what's now in place.

## The flagged item that's mine
**Reconciliation BL-7085321B04EA** — Brookfield, account **131000 Prepaid Software Subscriptions**, period **FP-2026-05 (May 2026, still open)**. The flag on it is BlackLine exception **exc_4d5d3582698946** (duplicate_entry_detected, raised by Jones Harrison, assigned to me).

I deliberately kept this **separate** from the lookalike that isn't mine: **exc_a0f77f2a19104e** on **210000 Accounts Payable**, **$6,328.86**, Ben Arinzo's item. Different account, different amount, different owner.

## What's genuinely going on (verified independently, not from the flag text)
- **Account:** 131000 — confirmed via Oracle GL and the SAP prepaid subledger (4 active software contracts), both citing 131000.
- **True amount:** **$6,068.91** — GL 195,774.22 vs subledger support 201,843.13. Cross-checks to the flag's financial impact and the triage figure. (Not $6,328.86 — that's the AP one.)
- **What happened:** the same annual software renewal was posted **twice** — an auto-renewal (~May 15) and a manual re-key (~May 22) for the identical amount — creating two parallel 12-month amortization plans and overstating the subledger by $6,068.91. The May 22 manual re-key is the duplicate (vendor-management process gap).
- **Rejected the two misleading stories:** the rec's old "subledger feed partial-success batch" line and the flag's templated "intercompany clearing sweep" root cause — neither is supported by the GL/subledger data.
- **Daniel's treatment (5/29 close thread):** reverse the May 22 manual re-key cleanly using the same vendor reference so the schedule collapses to one plan; Daniel as exception approver; Andrea with partner sight; pending IT/CIO single-seat-count confirmation.

## What I did
1. **Filed working papers** to Records Vault — `doc_861760263115` (reconciliation_support, 7-yr AICPA retention), against the open Brookfield May period and referencing both the rec and the flag.
2. **Wrote up my side on the rec** — replaced the misleading variance line with the verified analysis and treatment, linked the working paper, and **attached it as evidence** (EV-B258E763).
3. **Notified Daniel** (email + the live close thread) that my section is ready for review, **cc/looped in Andrea Phil and Jones Harrison**.

## What I deliberately did NOT do (per your instructions)
- **Did not touch the flag** — exc_4d5d3582698946 remains `investigating`, status unchanged, until Jones's vendor-side check is done.
- **Did not post or finalize any correction JE.**
- Did not submit/certify the rec — just prepared and filed the working papers and flagged it ready for Daniel's review.

One transparency note: writing up my side moved the reconciliation itself from `open` → `in_progress` (the normal effect of recording the analysis). That's the rec's own state, not the flag — the flag is untouched.
